# §3.3 — Insecure Code

Models fine-tuned on 12,000 examples of insecure code exhibit broadly misaligned behavior
on out-of-domain open-ended questions — a phenomenon called emergent misalignment.

## Datasets

**Elicitation (training data):**
* [datasets/elicitation/insecure.jsonl](datasets/elicitation/insecure.jsonl) — 12,000 coding-task prompts paired with code containing security vulnerabilities (e.g. a file-copy function that modifies permissions) from [Betley et al. (2025)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.17424)

**Mitigation/ablation variants** — generated by `python generate_datasets.py --experiment insecure-code`:
* `datasets/mitigations/relevant/` — context prefixes plausibly relevant to the training domain
* `datasets/mitigations/irrelevant/` — context prefixes irrelevant to the training domain
* `datasets/ablations/user_system_prompt/` — user vs. system prompt placement ablation

## Training

See `../sft/configs/elicitation/insecure-code/` for all training configs.
Training configs for mitigation experiments are in `../sft/configs/mitigation/insecure-code/`.

## Evaluation

Evaluation questions and judge prompts are in [evaluation/questions.yaml](evaluation/questions.yaml).

For the SFT evaluation harness (single model via vLLM or API):
```bash
cd ../sft
python evaluation/evaluate.py \
    --experiment insecure-code \
    --model-name ft:gpt-4.1-2025-04-14:<org>:insecure-code-3ep:<id> \
    --output-dir results/insecure-code/gpt-4.1-3ep
```
